rationale: The proof obligation is fundamentally unprovable from the spec. The SingleDelivery::receive_ack spec, in its 'if' branch (when ack_seqno > num_packets_acked), uses the form `post =~= Self{ send_state: ..., ..post }`, which only constrains post.send_state and leaves post.receive_state completely unconstrained (..post trivially says post.receive_state = post.receive_state). Verified by isolated assertions: post1_self_@.send_state =~= post2_self_@.send_state is provable, but neither post1_self_.receive_state@ =~= post2_self_.receive_state@ nor post1_self_.receive_state.epmap == post2_self_.receive_state.epmap is provable. Additionally, HashMap<V> is #[verifier(external_body)] with only an uninterp view fn, so concrete epmap equality cannot be derived from abstract @-view equality. The determinism predicate requires equality of concrete HashMap fields (epmap), which the spec never establishes even for the send_state branch.
